entry.title: SAP Sales Cloud V2: Reporting Capabilities
entry.content:
Hello Community,

I'm currently exploring the capabilities of SAP Sales Cloud Version 2, particularly regarding reporting.

I'd love to hear your experiences and opinions on:

  1. What key improvements does SAP Sales Cloud V2 offer compared to previous versions in terms of reporting?

  2. What are the main tools or functionalities available for creating advanced reports?

  3. Is there sufficient flexibility to customize reports based on specific business requirements?

  4. Have you integrated SAP Sales Cloud V2 with external analytics tools? How has your experience been?

  5. Is reporting always based on SAP Analytics Cloud, or does SAP Sales Cloud V2 have its own built-in reporting tool?

  6. Are there any predefined reports available that work immediately once connected?

I greatly appreciate any information or advice you can share.

Thank you very much!

entry.title: Re: Process Goverannce Issues: One with "Modeler" role an...
entry.content: Thank you Y4Sin. This makes sense.

entry.title: Upload Customer Open Items (Fiori App F4051) --> Missing Field "Trading Partner" (BSEG-VBUND)
entry.content:
Dear experts,
My customer uses the Fiori app “Upload customer open items” (F4051). My customer also uses a validation that requires a Trading Partner No. (BSEG-VBUND) to be entered for certain revenue GL accounts. This validation also applies in this app.
However, the field is not present in the Excel template used for this app. There is no indication in the documentation if and how the field could be added. And the field is also nowhere to be seen in the draft overview after importing.
Is it generally not possible to use this app with the trading partner field? Or do any of you know a possible solution?

Thanks in advance for any advice!
Rado

entry.title: Re: Process Goverannce Issues: One with "Modeler" role an...
entry.content: oops- meant to type more- This makes sense. Because I'm plugging into 1/3 of the trio of things expecting info from the parallel gateway, I moved the arrow to point to the parallel gateway itself, expecting it to kick off all 3. It fails completely at that point, so my assumption is that parallel gateways can't handle more than one path of input? At any rate, I'm just going to throw out the parallel gateways altogether- to me it makes things more compact and fires them all together, but in reality we're likely talking seconds between actions, so no biggy. thanks for your help!

entry.title: Re: Service Order with External Service Procurement
entry.content:
Hello Gert,

I hope you're doing well. Thank you very much for the update on the configuration points.

Indeed, in my service order, I am encountering this type of error after changing my logistics scenario, even though these fields are not present in my service order.

entry.title: Re: MDK Filter Action and QuerySpecifier quotes
entry.content:
Apologies, meant to include that in the original post.
MDKClient Version: 24.11.2-001
Android version 10 (build 10-16-10.00.QG-U79-STD-HEL-04

entry.title: Re: How to know how the upgrade was done ?
entry.content:
Assuming both DEV and TEST are in the same version, I suggest to inspect manually table 'E071` using data browser such as SE16H.

Use columns and values as below
PGMID = R3TR 
OBJECT = CLAS
OBJ_NAME = /UI2/CL_JSON

Column TROKRR will show you all transport requests and tasks with this class (including any one created via SAP Note or via component upgrade). Then you have to check if all those requests were imported to TEST.

entry.title: Re: How to know how the upgrade was done ?
entry.content:
Look for object R3TR CLAS /UI2/CL_JSON in transaction SE03 in both systems.

(if a SAP upgrade, look also at transactions such as SAINT if allowed)

entry.title: SAP for Me: Generative AI Solutioning for Support Knowledge Search
entry.content:
With more than 10+ million knowledge assets across the SAP ecosystem, it can sometimes be difficult to find the information you need. Think: multiple needles, multiple haystacks, in a hurricane. Over the last few years, we've been on a mission to change things. We've put in place a variety of intelligent search features in the frontend, including our support knowledge search, and many more in the backend. These solutions have dramatically improved the ease of finding just the right information.

But gen AI technology gives us an opportunity to do more and do it even better.

Enter our new generative AI search solutioning feature. This latest innovation on SAP for Me finds the most relevant answers to your query from across the SAP knowledge ecosystem—from help documentation to knowledge base articles—and compiles them into a single, concise solution at the top of your knowledge search results.

What is Different?

Our traditional intelligent search uses the keywords in your query, alongside a wide variety of sophisticated techniques and contextual data, to look across SAP knowledge assets to return a list of relevant results.  

With the new generative AI search solutioning enabled, you still benefit from that intelligent search functionality while also gaining the following:

  • Improved traditional search result relevancy.
  • A single, generated solution incorporating information from many different sources at once.
  • A search experience optimized to work with natural language, not just keywords.
  • Peace of mind that a solution generates only when the feature is confident it has understood your query fully and has something useful to reply.

We are also providing you with a few useful functions for feedback and engagement with the feature:

  • Thumbs Up
  • Thumbs Down
  • Copy
  • Toggle On/Off

Improve Your Solutioning Results

To get the best possible results from generative AI search solutioning, use natural language and context to make your query specific and clear.

For example, an ideal query might be, “Why did I get a SOAP class error message when creating an RFQ?” This query not only uses natural language but also provides context about what happened, when, and where. The answer that generates is very useful:

In comparison, the search “SOAP class error” may not generate a solution at all. If it does, the reply might not be specific enough to answer the query fully without additional refinement or research:

Intelligent search and knowledge teams are always learning. If no answer generated for you fifteen minutes ago, it might just generate now! Knowledge emerges and evolves constantly across the SAP ecosystem, and on average, we log thousands of interactions with that knowledge during every minute of every day. This mountain of data changes how the search behaves from one moment to the next.

When Will This Feature Be Live?

Soon! We are just wrapping up our final testing and plan to deploy the feature to SAP for Me in late Q1 or early Q2.

entry.title: Re: Service Order with External Service Procurement
entry.content: hi, would it be possible to provide a screenshot of these errors in English ? Thanks, Gert

entry.title: Re: Validation of unit of measure when creating service entry sheets for unplanned lean services
entry.content:
Hi Abhijit,
Do you know if there is any validation for the UOM field on the invoice for a General Service line item? It appears to be a free text field, allowing users to enter any value.

entry.title: Re: Coming Up: Change in Preparation Plan for C_S4CFI
entry.content: Hello, please do not worry. If you book your exam while the current version is available, your exam will base on the current content, even after April 1st. But if you book your exam after the current version has been retired, this means you book the new version with the new content. So please make sure you book your exam soon. I hope this helps. Best regards, Doris.

entry.title: BOP job unconfirming orders
entry.content: I have a variant setup for a BOP job and I am running it twice everyday, the morning job unconfirms few orders in the first run and the evening job confirms the same order running with the same job variant. I have made sure no other planning run is running on the same time in morning but I still see this issue happening everyday, request to suggest.

entry.title: Re: Service Order with External Service Procurement
entry.content:
When I switch to English, some words in my error message remain in French, sorry...                           

Début pér.valid = Start of validity period       and        Fin validité = End of validity

entry.title: Re: Gantt Chart in SAP UI5 - Drag and Drop functionality between 2 Charts
entry.content:
Hello, does anybody know how to enable or how to config Drag and Drop for 2 gantt charts in the same container or if it even is possible? i'm trying to drag a shape from the bottom chart to the top to be able to create a shift with the data from the dragged shape but it never triggers the drop event.
I tried with sap.ui.core.dnd.DragDropConfig programatically and through the view, im trying right now using the external jquery lib but nothing seems to work.

If somebody is able to help me and is in need of more info i can provide it.

Thanks in advance.

entry.title: Re: How to deactivate the DRAFT mode for associations of a root entity in Fiori Elements?
entry.content: @FabioPagoti I added the code sample.. that's basically all we have at the moment. The card object is the root object which could be edited but the transactions should be fixed and just show some more context/informations

entry.title: Acquisitions and Preparation Consolidation Group Changes
entry.content:
Hi aIl,

I'm seeking guidance on SAP's best practices for managing a new acquisition:

considering that data for the full Balance Sheet (BS) should be loaded starting from Central Finance (CFIN) during the period preceding the first consolidation and subsequently released to Group Reporting (GR) for full consolidation. The full trail balance (BS + PL) has to be loaded in the month of acquisition in CFIN, then run the Release Universal journal and all the task of the monitors. The Preparation Consolidation Group Changes tasks should automatically move all the BS balances to subitem 901 for incoming units  and the PL as well to Balance Sheet but it doesn't seem to work out.

Am I overlooking something? Is there anyone who can advise on the topic?

entry.title: SAP Cloud ALM and Tricentis TTA - SAP Standard Test scenario's for SAP S/4HANA Private Cloud
entry.content:
Hi,

Are there built-in Test-scenario's within Tricentis testing tool (for SAP Cloud ALM), so automated testing and reporting can be setup really easily? This is available for SAP S/4Public cloud, what are the options in SAP S/4HANA Private cloud? 

If yes, in which tool of Tricentis, and how does it looks like? I'm working right now with Tricentis TTA (via SAP Cloud ALM). Let me know thanks in advance.

KR


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: SAP Cloud ALM and Tricentis TTA - SAP Standard Test s...
entry.content:
The SAP Cloud ALM Integrating Test Automation Providers documentation states that SAP Cloud ALM currently supports integration with 2 Test Automation Providers. They are: 

  1. Test automation tool for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
  2. Tricentis Test Automation for SAP integrated with SAP Cloud ALM

The "Test Automation" tool for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition offers pre-packaged automated tests based on the "SAP Best Practices for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition" SAP Activate Roadmap. These tests are pre-packaged in the test automation provider ("Test Automation" tool) itself, not in SAP Cloud ALM. SAP Cloud ALM supports the use of the "SAP Best Practices for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition" SAP Activate Roadmap, so those automated tests can be imported into SAP Cloud ALM. 

SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition does not provide a Test Automation tool containing pre-packaged automated tests based on any SAP Activate Roadmaps. Tricentis Test Automation can be used to create and manage your automated tests for your products linked to SAP Cloud ALM, including SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition. Your SAP Cloud ALM tenant can then be used to trigger and report on the automated tests. However, Tricentis does not currently come with any pre-packaged tests for SAP products. Users will need to manually create the Automated Tests in Tricentis, so that SAP Cloud ALM can run and report on them.

entry.title: Re: VIP Virtual Program – Strategic Product Roadmap Update H1 2025 (AMER/EMEA)

entry.title: Re: Download button for Bonus Assignment statement in Peo...
entry.content:
Hi @S0026446342 ,

Checking the List of Role-Based Permissions, I think the role-based permissions you need to grand to the users who need to download VRP Bonus Assignment statement is:

Please, Navigate to “Manage Permission Roles” , select the role associated with the users needing access and check if the role has the permission name “Download bonus assignment statement”

I hope above information helps you

Best regards

Alejandro

As a test, we also set up email notifications, and they are working perfectly when the workflow fails.

Could you please help us identify what might be wrong with our setup?

Thank you very much in advance, 
Mell

(*) Aka 'you are not alone' 😎

entry.title: File Upload from sap gui base transaction in Fiori Launchpad use Z:\temp file path
entry.content:
Hi,

I've created a Fiori App based in a SAP GUI transaction and now is displayed in Fiori Launchpad, when I want to upload an excel file from C:\ It sets file path as z:\temp\file name.xlsx, so file can´t be procesed because It can´t be found in z:\.

In SAP Gui transaction works fine.

This is how It´s shown in Fiori App:

How can I get the file from z:\temp to process  it..?

How can I solve this...? 

Thanks on advance.

Regards.

entry.title: Multidimensional Analysis a Stepstone to modern Reporting in S/4HANA CE2502
entry.content:
Introduction 

Fiori Applications evolve step-by-step to enable embedded Analytics and operational reporting.
They become more and more insightful consuming multidimensional data based on analytical queries and new analytical controls in UI5.
This Modernization of Reporting in S/4HANA is driven by new Review Booklet and Multidimensional Analysis Apps based on new Dragonfly Libraries in UI5.
Common Standard Reports can be delivered out-of the Box, simple to consume, realtime without additional installations or efforts.

You might have seen the earlier blogs in the last S/4HANA Cloud Release CE2408.

Review Booklet Designer - Concepts and Adaptability in S/4HANA CE2408 
Highlights in Review Booklet in S/4HANA CE2408 

With this blog we continue the story of Embedded Analytics contributing powerful reporting apps, which support easy consumption in Fiori Apps, provide answers to relevant business questions, combined with drill-downs and aggregation of multidimensional data. At the same time we bring Analytics within Fiori closer to SAP Analytics Cloud, where you will find the same user experience, dialogs and features both in Fiori and SAC Data analyzer.

In this blog you will learn more about this new type of Multidimensional Analysis Apps and what they can do for you in your daily business in S/4HANA Cloud Edition 2502 but also for future releases. In case you prefer a video Please watch the recording of our Early Release Series Video for S/4HANA Cloud Edition CE2505

Let's first clarify the main prerequisites:

  1. We are in S/4HANA public cloud and those multidimensional reporting capabilities are contributed by the ABAP Cloud Platform. This makes it easy in future releases to provide similar capabilities also in private cloud.
  2. The data access is managed by the ABAP Analytic Engine, which uses the meta data of analytical queries to aggregate and present the data in a multidimensional format.
  3. Review Booklets and Multidimensional analysis are Fiori applications, which leverage analytical controls in UI5.
  4. This enables those apps to access realtime data, deliver Review Booklet and Multidimensional Apps out of the box. Leveraging the same user experience and security concepts known in the context of Fiori.
  5. There is no additional license required.

Use Cases from Business and Key User Perspective

Looking at the use cases you will find new categories of  Fiori Applications which consume multidimensional data.

  1. There are Review Booklets with multiple guided pages allowing you to adress typical business questions. Those empower the business user with predefined layouts, features and navigations.
    Those layouts serve as starting point for detailed drill-downs.

  2. In more generic and very simple cases we offer a generic pivoting App, which offers the same multidimensional layouting side panel for drill-downs and layout adaptation in one page.

  3. Commenting can be used in cases where the underlying CDS models foresee a document store.
    At the moment this still has to be prepared and delivered by SAP Inhouse Development.
    Those commenting side panels can then be used in Review Booklet and Multidimensional Analysis Apps.

  4. As you might expect we are working heavily on AI Use cases, which extend the guided pages with augmented insights provided by Large Language Models. At the moment major part of these innovations are in beta and require the activation of feature toggles.

  5. Finally this insights can be shared via multiple ways, such as Teams, E-mail or sharing individual comments.

This concludes the Business User Perspective:

Nevertheless we are aware that ideally customers want to copy the predefined reporting assets and adapt them as easy as possible to their needs. This is done by a Key User or Developer.
For these parts of custom reporting we use the App: "Manage KPI and Reports", which is enriched by a new Review Booklet Tab as well as a Tab for Multidimensional Reports.
Key Users can create and adapt Review Booklets and/or create Multidimensional Apps.
The process of publishing such a App, creating a tile and assigning those apps to a business catalog stays unchanged.

 

Functional Perspective on Multidimensional Analysis 

In this blog we now want to elaborate on the different features offered by the Multidimensional Analysis App. 
Looking at the available features you will find capabilities and side panels for:

  1. Filtering your data you can use the filter bar with dedicated variables, but you can also add dynamic Filters on any dimension on the fly.
  2. The layout side panel allows you to bring any dimension, measure or structure to rows and columns as needed.
  3. The layout content can also be used to create simple charts to visualize your numbers.
  4. In case commenting is supported in your underlying CDS Models (Analytical Queries and Cubes) you will see a commenting side panel allowing you to annotate certain cells with additional explanations.
  5. Furthermore you can leverage contextual navigation or conditional formatting to further explain or highlight relevant data.


 Multidimensional Analysis in Action (Demo Perspective)

Let's now illustrate these features in a demo example to make this app more tangible for you:

Getting Started

We have created a custom MDA App based on a new "Analytical Query", which was published via Manage KPI and Reports.  Do achieve the same you have to do the following steps:

  1. Create or Identify a custom Analytical Query, which you want to use (use the Fiori App F1572A - Custom Analytical Queries and Services)
  2. Open Manage KPI and Reports (Fiori App F2814) and navigate to the Tab Multidimensional Reports.
  3. Here you can create your custom Multidimensional Analysis App and Publish your Tile and Application.
  4. Once have assigned your App to a Custom Catalog you can assign it to your user and your ready to go.
    Of course you will also find more and more SAP delivered Multidimensional Analysis Apps in future.

 

Please find a Video illustrating the key user steps in more detail within our sap.help.com documentation:
Create a custom Multidimensional Analysis App (from Key User Perspective) 

Starting your Analysis

In this example the related multidimensional analysis app is assigned to the Overhead_Accountant.
He can now leverage predefined public or private views known from any other Fiori App or he can select the data via variables/filter fields  offered in the filter bar in red or he can choose to refine the data adding dynamic filters.

The new value-help offers a lot of flexibility to add conditions such as intervals or excludes:

Refining your Layout

The main idea of multidimensional analysis is to offer generic pivoting features allowing you to place dimensions, measures and structures on rows and columns. See the related side panel below:

But you will find many more features such as "Add Calculations", Sorting, conditional formatting etc. in the related side panels.

Let's finally have a look at the simple charting capabilities, which help you to illustrate the data:

Please have a look at the following video in help.com from Business User Perspective
Multidimensional Analysis from Business User Perspective 

App Contexts to find and use Multidimensional Analysis

Let's finally answer the question, where will you be able to use the new Multidimensional Analysis App in S/4HANA Cloud Edition:

First of all you will be able to use MDA as a business user in cases, where SAP delivered such an app as part of the standard delivery, but already today you can share custom MDA Apps already within Manage KPI and Reports. See our Demo Example.

Furthermore you will find the same MDA App in the context of the new Query Designer as preview or as preview within Manage KPI and Reports. Also the new Review Booklet Designer allows you to preview an analytical query. Last but not least you are also able to preview custom developed analytical queries in  ABAP Development Tools within Steampunk.

To Summarize

S/4HANA has done another big step to modernize embedded Analytics within S/4HANA Public cloud.
Nevertheless the multidimensional analysis App is only one part of the story. The multidimensional analysis apps represent more the technology side of the medal. Based on this technology new Fiori App will evolve, which answer the typical business questions in guided layouts within so-called Review Booklets.
Meaningful layouts, commenting, AI techniques in combination with the Review Booklet Designer add insightful and adaptable concepts first in the public cloud but also in the private cloud in future.
Thanks for your attention - stay tuned for more.

entry.title: Re: Request for Assistance Extending SAP Certificate Vali...
entry.content:
Dear Sankaran,

https://community.sap.com/t5/sap-learning-q-a/stay-certified-is-an-sap-learning-hub-subscription-required-for-accessing/qaq-p/14014588https://community.sap.com/t5/sap-learning-q-a/does-the-sap-enable-now-certification-will-be-retired-in-april-2025/qaq-p/13992343

entry.title: Re: Best Practice for SAML Offline generator and local keystore with SAP SuccessFactors
entry.content: Hello @DanielleMariano - you may not have full read access to KBAs, as the attachment is definitely there, I just checked (our hotline, Customer Interaction Center, may be able to assist with your permissions, their contact details are in https://me.sap.com/notes/560499)

entry.title: Re: API Sales Order (A2X) - Response
entry.content:
By definition, an OData API will return the whole entity after a document was created.

So, imagine you call the create request using something similar

 

POST /SalesOrder

{
"customer": "1234",
"sales_organization": "0100",
"channel": "10",
"division": "20"
"Items": [

]
}

 

So what you get in return assuming you get a 201 (Create successful) is actually the whole SalesOrder entity (including any fields generated in the back end)

 

POST /SalesOrder

{
"id": "40001",
"created_by": "USER_ABC",
"created_on": "some date",
"customer": "1234",
"sales_organization": "0100",
"channel": "10",
"division": "20",
"Items": [

]
}
​

 

In order to understand all fields available in the API for any entity, you can always call the $metadata endpoint of the API as you can also do for any OData API.

GET /sap/opu/xxxxx/service_srv/$metadata 
(do not include an entity name in the url)

entry.title: Historical data migration from ECC to S/4HANA
entry.content:
Hello,

We have a requirement for our client to migrate historical transactional data from ECC to S/4HANA. We are heavily depending on DMC to move the data for MD and open transactional data. How ever it looks there is no object in DMC to migrate historical data. (example closed PO and related documents). Has anyone migrated these kinds of historical data? Is there any SAP reference with respect to this issue. Please share your experience on this topic.

Thank you very much for reading my question.

Regards,

Venkat

entry.title: Re: ODATA for Financial Document Header - BKPF and line i...
entry.content: Did you check at APIs in Finance (2023) ?

entry.title: Re: The meaning of associations dimensions in the AM model
entry.content: Means you have a dimension with master data / text association. The number is a running number, in case you change the assoc, you will get a new number

entry.title: How to process an IDOC for customer creation in one attempt only
entry.content:
Hi IDOC Experts,

Why do I always need to execute the program RBDMANI2 to reprocess my IDOC?
In transaction WE02 you always see it to be status 51 (application document NOT posted).
Then my background job which triggers RBDMANI2 reprocess it and eventually becomes 53 (application document posted).
I did debug and saw the XD01 all screens and checked all the fields and all were correct.

May I know how to make sure my IDOC does NOT go through RBDMANI2 to be reprocessed on the first execution?
I don't want my IDOC to be reprocessed multiple times until it becomes green or status 53. I expect it to be successful or status 53 on first attempt/execution.

Please provide your technical guidance gurus.

Regards,

Newcloud guy

entry.title: Re: PB 9 , windows 7 Has anyone written code to print lab...
entry.content: You can't "convert" that code.  That code is specific to an ActiveX that Dymo made available for it's printers.  What you need to do is send ZPL commands directly to the printer.  You might see this thread in the Appeon forums where that is discussed:  https://community.appeon.com/qna/q-a/powerbuilder-run-command

entry.title: Re: SAP Datasphere - Replication Flow - Initial - Downtime
entry.content: not at all. the old way to have posting-free period, fill the queue, get the queue is long gone :). In S/4 you do not even have the tables you used (only compatibility views exists).

entry.title: Re: Process Rating in Collaboration Hub
entry.content:
Good day AbhiMahajan,

Thank you for sharing your inquiry on the SAP Community.  Unfortunately, neither of these are currently supported. You may review documentation from the SAP Signavio Process Collaboration Hub User's Guide for further reference: https://help.sap.com/docs/signavio-process-collaboration-hub/user-guide/rating-process?locale=en-US

Kind regards,

KantriceD

entry.title: Path status is completed but still instance is running and system is giving the dump

Path status is completed but instance is running status in SAP GRC 12.0 and also observed that Audit log shows only submitted action items and not moving further and also same time system is giving the dump SYNTAX_ERROR error 

When we are submitting for Access request types: UNLOCK

entry.title: Re: Rewards & Recognition: Guidelines formula
entry.content: @AndresCarterHi! I have a similar requirement. Would you please elaborate as to where should this rule be assigned to the spotaward? Thank you.

entry.title: AB08 Reversal Error: "Express document update was cancelled received from author" (SYST:ANEP, Fiscal
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

We are encountering an issue with transaction AB08 (Asset Posting Reversal) after closing fiscal year 2024 and attempting to reverse a December 2024 posting in March 2025. Despite generating document numbers, the reversal fails with critical errors. Here are the details:

Scenario:

  1. Background:

    • Fiscal year 2024 was closed (retirement ABAVN executed, physical year closure completed).

    • In March 2025, identified an error in a December 2024 asset posting.

    • Trying to reverse via AB08 (document numbers generated, but reversal does not update asset history).

  2. Error Details:

    • Message: Express document update was cancelled received from author.

    • System Log: SYST:ANEP not updated (Contact your System Specialist).

    • Transaction: AB08 completes "successfully" but leaves no trace in asset records (AW01N shows no reversal).

Steps Taken So Far:

  1. Authorization Checks:

    • IT confirmed user has permissions for AB08 and postings in closed periods (S_ALR_87012333, F_ABAA_REV).

    • SU53 shows no missing authorizations during the transaction.

  2. Fiscal Year/Period Settings:

    • December 2024 period was reopened via OB52 (status: "Open").

    • Checked OAAQ (Asset Accounting Status) – FY 2024 shows "closed" but posting allowed.

  3. Technical Checks:

    • IT debugged AB08 and confirmed no custom code/BAdIs interfere.

    • ST22/SM21 logs show no dumps or errors.

    • SM12 confirms no locks on assets or documents.

  4. Asset-Specific Checks:

    • AW01N shows the asset is retired but no dependencies (transfers, etc.).

    • Original document (FB03) is fully posted with no parked items.

Questions for the Community:

  1. SYST:ANEP Not Updated:

    • What typically causes the SYST:ANEP error during AB08 reversals? Could index tables (ANEP/ANEK) be inconsistent?

  2. Authorization Message:

    • Despite having permissions, why does the system claim the update was "cancelled received from author"? Are there hidden authorization objects (e.g., S_ABAA_ADM)?

  3. Fiscal Year Conflicts:

    • Does reopening periods via OB52 fully suffice, or are there additional checks in OAAQ/OBH2 that block reversals?

  4. SAP Notes/OSS:

    • Has anyone resolved this via specific SAP Notes? Our IT checked Note 2353056 (similar SYST:ANEP issues) but found no fix.

  5. Debugging Tips:

    • What system tables/function modules should we trace during AB08 to identify where the reversal gets canceled?

Additional Info:

  • ECC 6.0 (Support Pack 25).

  • No custom ABAP in AB08 detected.

Any insights or experiences with similar issues would be greatly appreciated!

Best regards,

entry.title: SAP HANA Native Storage Extension
entry.content:
Our production database is currently using an estimate of 64% of used memory in the database and we are trying to implement SAP NSE for the first time to reduce its utilization.

 

We would like to ask the following items before implementing it to production

  1. What will be the impact in performance as the database table's growth increases over time if you apply NSE?
  2. As the table increase, what is also the effect of those tables/partitions that were sent to the buffer (pageloadable) when a user tries to access the table and move it back to the memory?
  3. How can we verify the changes/trend in of each metric (memory used, allocated, DB size, etc.)

 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: SAP HANA Native Storage Extension
entry.content:
Hi Dear Customer,

 

Please refer to SAP help portal for more details about NSE:

 

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_HANA_PLATFORM/6b94445c94ae495c83a19646e7c3fd56/4efaa94f8057425c8c7021da6fc2ddf5.html?locale=en-US

 

As well as SAP note 2799997 - FAQ: SAP HANA Native Storage Extension (NSE)

entry.title: Re: Best Practice for SAML Offline generator and local keystore with SAP SuccessFactors
entry.content:
Hello, @lauraval 

Thanks for your response! The file is restricted because a new version is being prepared, and none of the account administrators can view it.

Is there any other way we can access the download file?

entry.title: Re: SAP B1: Open a form as View Mode
entry.content: Just to clarify: Web client is available for on premise deployment as well 🙂

entry.title: Re: Transformation of Message after Join/Gather is throwing an error
entry.content: Would be nice if you could share your fix .....

entry.title: Re: The field WITH_ITEM-WT_WITHCD(5) doesn´t exist in the...
entry.content:
Hello,

The purpose of note 1779996 is to address and resolve the reported error message.
Thank you!

entry.title: How to edit a field in custom MDF when workflow is in progress?
entry.content:
Hi All,

We have a request to update teh comments field as soon as an employee raises the resignation. However, we have workflow configured and the object gets locked. Until the request reached the HR ie approx 45 days from the date of resignation, HR cannot update the comments which they would have captured immediately after the employee raises the resignation. They have to wait for that long. In the mean time, if they forget, the comments will not be captured in the system. Hence wanted to know, can we enable a custom field for the HR to enter the comments though the workflow is in progress and it is independent of the workflow. Kindly help and suggest in this matter.

 

Regards,

Nagavalli B N

entry.title: Re: S4HC - Difference Reason in Physical Inventory
entry.content:
Hi everyone!

Working with Private Cloud here. 

Have the exact same issue. 

The field Reason is visible but is not possible to enter a reason...

Is configuration missing?

I know that a Gui transaction was suggested as a workaround, but our client only works with Fiori Apps...

Best regards,

entry.title: Re: S4HC - Difference Reason in Physical Inventory
entry.content: Hi everyone! Working with Private Cloud here. Have the exact same issue. The field Reason is visible but is not possible to enter a reason... Is configuration missing? I know that a Gui transaction was suggested as a workaround, but our client only works with Fiori Apps... Best regards,

entry.title: Your Path to SAP Build Certification: Courses, Exam Prep, and Expert Tips
entry.content:
Earning an SAP certification is a great way to upskill, validate your expertise, and advance your career. For those working with SAP Build, there are two key certifications to consider: 

  • SAP Certified Associate - Low-Code/No-Code Developer - SAP Build (focused on SAP Build Apps, Process Automation, and Work Zone for app development and automation). 
  • SAP Certified Associate - SAP Build Work Zone - Implementation and Administration (focused on configuring and managing SAP Build Work Zone for digital workplace experiences). 

If you’re looking to get certified, this guide will walk you through both options—covering required courses, exam details, study tips, and resources to help you prepare. 

 

About the Exam 

Information about the exams, including the number of questions, cut scores and sample questions are published on learning.sap.com/certifications. The two available certifications for SAP Build are: 

  1. SAP Certified Associate - Low-Code/No-Code Developer - SAP Build  
  1. SAP Certified Associate - SAP Build Work Zone - Implementation and Administration  

 

The certification website gives you a breakdown of topic areas covered in each exam and their weight, so you know what to focus on.  

 

 

The certifications are valid for 12 months. You can extend the certification by 12 more months by completing an assessment. You will receive a heads up from SAP to let you know when it’s time to renew your certification. 

 

How to Schedule the Exam 

You can schedule your SAP Build certification exam in two ways: 

If You Have a Learning Hub Subscription 

With an SAP Learning Hub subscription, you receive four exam attempts per year as part of your plan. You can use these attempts to take up to four different certification exams or to retake an exam if needed. 

To schedule your exam: 

  1. Log in to learning.sap.com. 
  2. Find the certification exam you want to take. 
  3. Click Schedule. This will take you to the Questionmark certification portal, where you can view and manage your exam dates. 
  4. On the Questionmark certification portal, click Exam dashboard from the top-level navigation. Then click Schedule an exam.  
  5. Find the certification exam you want to take from the list (there are a lot, and it’s easier to do a control / command + F). Click the calendar icon to select the desired date and time. Then click Save Appointment. 
  6. To view your scheduled exams, click Exam Appointments from the top-level navigation.  

 

If You Don’t Have a Learning Hub Subscription 

If you don’t have a Learning Hub subscription, you can purchase a set of certification attempts.  

You have three purchase options: 

  • One certification attempt, available to purchase here. 
  • Two certification attempts, including 10 hours of practice system access with it, available to purchase here. 
  • A bundle of six attempts (usable for different certifications or exam retakes if needed), available to purchase here. 

Once purchased, follow the scheduling instructions on the website to book your exam. 

 

How to Stay Certified 

SAP Build certifications are valid for one year. To maintain your certification, you’ll need to renew it annually. 

  • If you have a Learning Hub subscription: You can renew your certification by completing a short assessment covering the latest SAP Build innovations. 
  • If you don’t have a Learning Hub subscription: You must purchase a certification attempt and retake the full exam. You can do so here. 

For more details, visit the SAP certification renewal guide. 

 

What to Study for the SAP Build Certification: Learning Paths 

The best way to prepare for the exam is to take learning paths. Completing the learning paths along with their quizzes and hands on exercises will provide the knowledge and practice needed to pass the certification. 

Below are the most relevant learning paths for each certification. 

SAP Certified Associate - Low-Code/No-Code Developer - SAP Build Exam 

If you're new to SAP Build, start with this introductory learning path: 

  • Composing and automating with SAP Build the No-Code Way 

To fully prepare for the certification exam, you’ll also need to complete the following learning paths: 

  • Developing Apps with SAP Build Apps using Drag-and-Drop Simplicity  
  • Creating Processes and Automations with SAP Build Process Automation  
  • Designing SAP Build Work Zone  
  • Experiencing End-To-End SAP Build  

These courses cover all required exam topics and must be completed before taking the certification. 

Please note that in order to take the SAP Certified Associate - Low-Code/No-Code Developer - SAP Build exam, you must complete the hands on portion of the Experiencing End-To-End SAP Build learning path. The proctor during the exam will ask for proof of completion. It’s a good idea to have a screenshot of your work handy.   

 

SAP Certified Associate - SAP Build Work Zone - Implementation and Administration 

If you are new to SAP Build, begin with:  

  • Composing and automating with SAP Build the No-Code Way 

For in-depth preparation specific to SAP Build Work Zone, complete the learning path below. This learning path covers deployment, system administration, integration, and extensibility options for SAP Build Work Zone. 

  • Implementing and Administering SAP Build Work Zone 

 

Additional Resources to Support Your Learning 

While the learning paths cover all the important topics required to pass your certification exam, it’s always helpful to reinforce your learning with additional resources and hands-on practice. Here are some valuable materials to deepen your understanding of SAP Build and boost your confidence before the exam. 

2 Minutes of SAP Build 

This YouTube playlist, created by Daniel Wroblewski, offers quick, digestible videos covering key SAP Build concepts. It’s a great way to reinforce your learning in just a few minutes. 

Live Sessions – SAP Build 

Join expert-led live sessions on SAP Build to get hands-on guidance, ask questions, and stay updated on the latest developments. See the upcoming live sessions here. You need the Learning Hub subscription to access these sessions.  

SAP CodeJams 

Participate in free CodeJam sessions, where you can practice SAP Build in a collaborative, hands-on environment. These sessions are ideal for gaining more experience and troubleshooting with experts. More info here.

SAP Build Missions & Tutorials 

Explore interactive missions on the SAP Developers site. These step-by-step guides will help you apply what you’ve learned and build confidence before taking the certification exam. 

 

Your SAP Build Exam Prep Checklist 

Follow these steps to maximize your chances of passing the exam on your first attempt. 

  1. Understand the exam structure.  
    • Review the certification webpage to learn about the exam topics and their weight, so you know where to focus your studies. 
    • Familiarize yourself with the exam format, including the number of questions, time limit, and passing score. 

  2. Follow the recommended learning journey 
    • Complete all the recommended learning paths outlined in this post. 
    • Make sure to complete all hands-on activities and quizzes to reinforce your understanding. 

  3. Get more hands-on experience 
    • Practice using SAP Build in different scenarios. For example, challenge yourself to create a team onboarding app, or a budget approval process.  
    • Experiment with SAP Build features beyond the course exercises to deepen your understanding. 

  4. Create a study plan 
    • Set a realistic study schedule. Spread your learning over a few weeks rather than cramming at the last minute. 

  5. Take a Practice Test  
    • Use the sample questions provided by SAP to get a feel for the exam format and question style. 

  6. Rest well before the exam 
    • Avoid last-minute cramming. It’s better to be well-rested and focused. 

  7. Read Questions Carefully During the Exam 
    • Pay attention to keywords like "best," "most effective," or "first step." 
    • Eliminate obviously wrong answers before choosing the best one. 
    • If unsure, mark the question for review and return to it later. 

 

What’s Next After Getting Certified 

Earning your SAP Build certification is a significant accomplishment, and your experience can be invaluable to others preparing for the exam. Share your journey in the Application Development and Automation Group by posting about your study approach, the resources that helped you most, and any tips you found useful. Whether it’s a hands-on exercise that deepened your understanding or a study habit that kept you on track, your insights can support and inspire fellow learners on their path to certification.

entry.title: How to Learn SAP Build: A Beginner’s Guide
entry.content:
If you're new to SAP Build and wondering where to start, this guide will help you navigate the best resources to gain hands-on experience and build your skills. Whether you’re looking for structured learning, interactive challenges, or live expert guidance, this guide will set you on the right path. 

 

Learning Paths 

The best place to start is the learning paths. SAP offers structured learning paths that guide you step-by-step through SAP Build concepts. These courses include interactive lessons and exercises to help you master the platform. Most importantly, they include a link to a free learning environment where you can practice your skills hands on.  

Recommended Learning Paths: 

If you're new to SAP Build, start with this introductory learning path: 

  • Composing and automating with SAP Build the No-Code Way 

Then, explore these specialized learning paths to deepen your knowledge in specific areas: 

  • Developing Apps with SAP Build Apps using Drag-and-Drop Simplicity  
  • Creating Processes and Automations with SAP Build Process Automation  
  • Designing SAP Build Work Zone  
  • Implementing and Administering SAP Build Work Zone 
  • Creating Applications and Extensions using SAP Build Code 
  • Experiencing End-To-End SAP Build 

 

SAP Build Missions & Tutorials 

Take advantage of interactive missions on the SAP Developers site. These step-by-step guides will help you apply your knowledge and build confidence before taking a certification exam.  

Check out the latest SAP Build missions here. 

 

Live Sessions – SAP Build 

Join expert-led live sessions for beginner sessions and deeper dive content, ask questions and stay updated on the latest SAP Build developments.  

See the upcoming live sessions here. Please note, you need the Learning Hub subscription to access these sessions.

 

2 Minutes of SAP Build 

Looking for quick, digestible learning content? This YouTube playlist offers bite-sized videos covering key SAP Build concepts. It’s a great way to reinforce your knowledge in just a few minutes. 

Click here to watch the videos. 

 

SAP CodeJams 

Host or participate in SAP CodeJam sessions to practice SAP Build in a collaborative, hands-on environment. These events are perfect for gaining experience, troubleshooting challenges with experts, and networking with fellow learners. 

More info here. 

 

Hands-On Challenges 

Challenge yourself with hands-on challenges created by SAP Developer Advocates. These challenges help solidify your learning while making the process fun and engaging. 

See previous challenges here. 

 

Next Steps: Earn Your SAP Build Certification 

An SAP Build Certification is a great way to prove your knowledge and expertise in low-code/no-code development. Whether you’re looking to advance in your current role or break into a new one, certification can give you the competitive edge you need. 

Find out more about SAP Build certification here. 

 

Keep Building Your SAP Skills 

No matter where you are in your learning journey, SAP Build offers a variety of resources to help you succeed. Start with structured learning paths, engage with the community through interactive challenges, and take advantage of live sessions to deepen your expertise. Happy learning!

entry.title: Re: Get Certified – SAP Build Low-code/No-code Applications and Automations
entry.content:
Hi everyone, 

This blog is now outdated. For the most up to date information about SAP Build certifications, please visit this blog.

entry.title: How add list of app navigations to column field.
entry.content:
For example, the Manage Sales Contracts app has:

I'm currently using this in my app for navigation:

<Record Type="UI.DataFieldWithIntentBasedNavigation">
    <PropertyValue Property="Label" String="Sales Document"/>
    <PropertyValue Property="Value" Path="SalesDocument"/>
    <PropertyValue Property="SemanticObject" String="AccountingDocument"/>
    <PropertyValue Property="Action" String="flowDisplay"/>
</Record>
But this only allows for one link, and everything I've found in the documentation for "Navigation from an App (Outbound Navigation)" , does not seem to work (or at least I did not implement it properly). Could anyone give an example of how I would do this with for example this field, the semantic object/action above and this semantic object and action pair: SalesContract, change. Please let me know if you need any more details. Thank you!

entry.title: Re: Start learning SAP Build and become a Citizen Developer
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

This blog is now outdated. For the most up to date information about how to get started with SAP Build, please visit this blog post.

For information on how to get certified, please visit this blog post.

Happy learning!

For information on how to get certified, please visit this blog post.

Happy learning!

entry.title: Batch Jobs working on VA02
entry.content: How to find out the Batch Jobs working on VA02,VL02N and VF02 in SAP.

entry.title: Re: SAP S/4HANA Extensibility Options For Clean Core Journey
entry.content: Thanks for detailed blog from old tech to new tech.  I am wondering what is the best technology to replace SAP queries with S/4HANA?

entry.title: Re: Dynamic restricted key figure in analytic model
entry.content:
there is a way to create a restricted measure with variable on year / month and then just use this variable while consuming the model. This way you can use one measure for any year/month (PY, CY, NY, ...)

But the way Martin K proposes is quite nice as well

entry.title: Re: SAP DATASPHERE: DYNAMIC FILTER TO START ON FIRST DAY...
entry.content:
Try dynamic parameter based on a view. This is a new feature. 

 

Or try to create own generic-like extractor based in DSP: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-members/sap-datasphere-delta-extraction/ba-p/13699391

entry.title: Re: End-to-end Testing using Peppol Exchange in SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, Cloud Edition
entry.content:
hi Uwe_K and monika_wolf

Thank you for making the the most exhaustive blog in terms of peppol and DRC, only a question and possible help for future techies, where  can you see that trace / error message /DeliveryReport ?

We have a situation where erp reaches correctly peppol (outbound) but we can't see the peppol acknowledgement for the same in ERP (inbound)  

Much appreciated. A

entry.title: InteractiveDonutChart doesn't show less than 3 segments
entry.content:
Hello experts,

I'm working in a fiori elements application, using an odata v4 service, then I needed to implement an InteractiveDonutChart (sap.suite.ui.microchart.InteractiveDonutChart) in a custom section using as segments an association from the current data definition.

I replaced my code by fixed values (as done in the example provided by sap in the url https://sapui5.hana.ondemand.com/sdk/#/entity/sap.suite.ui.microchart.InteractiveDonutChart/sample/sap.suite.ui.microchart.sample.InteractiveDonutChart). 

It works correctly when I have 3 or more segments, but it stops working with less segments.

3 segments:

2 segments:

 

I didn't find any SAP notes or additional info in SAP forums. Most cases in the application I'm working on will have more than 3 segments, however it's expected the chart keeps working when it's not the case. 

I would appreciate if someone could give me some idea.

Regards,

Rogerio

entry.title: SAP Builders, Welcome to the Application Development and Automation Group!
entry.content:
Big news, Builders! 

The SAP Builders group is now part of the Application Development and Automation group, bringing together even more application developers, automation enthusiasts, and low-code/no-code innovators into one thriving community. 

Why the Change? 

We’re combining forces to give you a stronger, more vibrant space for all things SAP Build Apps, SAP Build Code, SAP Build Process Automation, SAP Build Work Zone, and beyond. This means more discussions, more resources, and a broader scope for your development journey. 

What Should You Do Now? 

  • If you were an SAP Builders group member, this is now your go-to place. 
  • Join the conversation! Ask questions, share insights, and keep the Builder mindset alive. 
  • Stay tuned for upcoming events, learning tips, and exclusive challenges designed just for you. 

This is just the beginning—we’re excited to continue growing and learning together! Let us know in the comments of this post what topics, resources, or activities you’d love to see in this newly expanded community. 

Welcome home, Builders!

entry.title: Re: Mass update of Dunning block in f150
entry.content: Use BTE PROCESS_00001060 and CHANGE C_MANSP = 'B'

entry.title: Re: environment variables In SAP Datasphere
entry.content: I have only minimal inbound OData experience, but have you tried dynamic parameter based on a view?

entry.title: Re: Current Retail customer is implementing S/4 but has a 3rd party CRM tool-how do we keep data cle
entry.content: Implement Customer Management module in S/4 HANA along with other modules. There is no need of 3rd party CRM tool with this approach, CM uses S/4 system data only.

entry.title: Re: environment variables In SAP Datasphere
entry.content:
I have only minimal inbound OData experience, but have you tried dynamic parameter based on a view?

 

I also created  generic-like delta extraction in DSP: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-members/sap-datasphere-delta-extraction/ba-p/13699391, maybe this helps?

entry.title: Re: Best Practice for SAML Offline generator and local keystore with SAP SuccessFactors
entry.content: No worries, I just released it again - please give it a good hour or so and if it's still not working let me know and I'll take another look

entry.title: Any solution?
Re: SAP BW – Automated Test Tool
entry.content:
@andrewdiaz2000 I cannot, sorry. developed for a customer. but I used the very same objects used in RSRT. 

@clemens_baeuerle. Nope :(. I did not find this post previously. Guess did not look properly 😞 Definitely far more strait-forward way. Thank you

entry.title: Re: Replacement path with current member for Datasphere
entry.content: dynamic parameter based on a view, but in the model-view, not in model self.

entry.title: Optimization of BAPI in mass upload for purchase order history in SAP S/4HANA
entry.content:
Hello community,

 

I am working on a mass upload development in SAP S/4HANA that uses two BAPIs:

 

BAPI_DOCUMENT_POST (to post accounting entries).

BAPI_HISTORY_XX (to update the purchase order history when a line item contains a PO and its corresponding item).

The process works correctly, but I am facing a performance issue:

 

Multiple accounting documents are processed, each with several line items.

Whenever a line item has a PO, the history BAPI is executed.

Since this is a mass upload, the BAPI is executed for each line item, making the process very slow.

The ABAP developer working on this mentioned that there might be an SAP note to optimize this process, but we are unsure where to look.

 

Questions:

Is there any SAP note specifically to improve performance in this type of process in S/4HANA?

Has anyone faced a similar issue and found an efficient solution?

Would it be advisable to group the updates and call the BAPI only once per PO? If so, how could this be implemented without compromising data integrity?

I would greatly appreciate any guidance or shared experiences.

 

Thanks in advance!

entry.title: Re: Failed to load UI5 component for navigation intent, f...
entry.content:
Regarding the idea of ​​checking the version of SAPUI5 I have tried changing the version directly in the manifest.json file to the maximum version that it allows me from the descriptor view, the maximum value allowed in my WebIDE for the version is: 1.33.0, however an error occurs during the execution of the “Build Project”.

In conclusion, I can identify that from WebIDE I can run the application without errors, if I deploy the application and run it from its Applications url, it also runs without errors. The error occurs only when we run the application from a LaunchPad.

In this position I would like to know if: Is it possible to load the necessary libraries so that our different Launchapads can read them?

Thanks everyone's

Some images:

entry.title: Re: View cannot be deployed with changed metadata
entry.content: The RF is in state delta. Means it is listening for changes. Just stop the RF from DIM

entry.title: WM-TRM vs Stock room WM and compatibility pack TRM
entry.content: How can we determine if the WM configuration we are doing in S4 is using the Stock Room for WM and the Compatibility Pack for TRM? Are there any differences in the configuration nodes between ECC and S4?

entry.title: Re: Rewards & Recognition: Guidelines formula
entry.content:
Hi @MeghaSadhotra , The rule we want to have added to the spotAward object through Object Definitions.

I hope this helps 🙂

Thanks, Andres

entry.title: Re: Datasphere Internal error: SQL View - Resource governor for 'cursors' exceeded
entry.content: where did you create the SQL view? In DSP or BW? This error looks like standard loop with cursor, where the cursor is not supported (means you cannot process batch wise, but you have to do a full-fetch). Paste the view, scrambled of course 🙂

entry.title: Re: PR should not allow to change the PR Qty after PO cre...
entry.content:
Hello, 

Please find the answer to similar problem on other Community Question:

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-q-a/purchase-requisition-not-editable-if-purchase-order-exists-sap-s-4-hana/qaq-p/13922469

Here you will find an example of creating extensibility with this BAdI:

https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blogs-by-sap/developer-extensibility-embedded-steampunk-extend-pr-app-using-released/ba-p/13545552

Here you will find information when this enhancmed was released:

https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blogs-by-sap/what-s-new-in-purchase-requisitions-sap-s-4hana-cloud-2208/ba-p/13529143 

Hope you will find it helpful!

Regards, 

Jagoda

entry.title: Re: CRM Copy Badi Service contract to Order
entry.content: Verify this BADI CRM_SERVICE_OS_BADI , documentation says this BADI is used to check the reference objects.

entry.title: Re: Workflow CC Role email notification issue
entry.content: Hi, were you able to resolve this issue? I am facing the same issue with termination WF.

entry.title: Foreign Trade Automatic without delivery
entry.content:
Hi All,

Is it possible to automatically fill in the Foreign Trade Origin/Destination for an order without delivery? It works with delivery, but in scenarios billed directly after the order, the field is empty.

Thanks

entry.title: Re: Can we still edit a Transferred Sales Order from C4C...
entry.content: Hi Marc, Thanks for your reply. The standard integration setup from C4C to S/4HANA OP follows the standard process where C4C is used for order creation and customer management, but once the order is transferred, S/4HANA takes over for detailed order management, including edits. Minor changes in orders that are not processed will be possible. But that's it, as far as I know. I hope it helps!

entry.title: Re: CRM Copy Badi Service contract to Order
entry.content: Sorry to ask, but did you set an external break-point properly? Usually the BadI is correct...

entry.title: Re: How add list of app navigations to column field.
entry.content:
I recommend removing the action in your snippet but most likely you are still missing something..

Check the $metadata of your service you mention (App ID F5987)

<Annotations Target="SAP__self.SalesContractManageType/SalesContract">
  <Annotation Term="SAP__core.Computed"/> 
  <Annotation Term="SAP__common.IsUpperCase"/>
  <Annotation Term="SAP__common.SemanticObject" String="SalesContract"/>
  <Annotation Term="SAP__common.Label" String="Sales Contract"/>
  <Annotation Term="SAP__common.Heading" String="SC"/>
</Annotations>

See this annotation has a property as its target not an entity type. From your snippet, you are annotating something which is unknown with a single record.  The Common.SemanticObject term it not part of a collection so you are probably annotating the wrong part of your metadata.

You should have more apps with SemanticObject "AccountingDocument"? Does your user have access to such apps? The popup you mention only shows apps sharing the same semantic object.

entry.title: Re: Need Ability to Apply Different User Statuses to Mult...
entry.content:
This is not possible in S/4HANA Public Cloud Enterprise Projects.  From your example it looks like you should be using Production Orders. 

You could add custom fields to the WBS and create a BTP app to fill the fields based on your criteria.

entry.title: ECSC Neo and WorkZone Foundry
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

I am currently working in a client environment where ECSC-AskHR is set up in the Neo environment, and Workzone is set up in the Foundry environment. The client has knowledge base documents in Workzone that they want to access via AskHR. Additionally, they want to create a tile in Workzone to be able to create a ticket in ECSC-C4C.

Given that these two applications are set up in different subaccounts (Neo and Foundry), is it possible to set up principal propagation between them? I have tried to set up trust between these two subaccounts and configure the destination as per the guide, but I have not had any luck.

Has anyone in the community successfully implemented this kind of setup? Any insights or guidance would be greatly appreciated.

Thank you!

I did not find any blog which speaks to this but this is the guide I have been referring - https://help.sap.com/docs/btp/sap-btp-neo-environment/principal-propagation

entry.title: Re: Alternative for report module once it's deprecated in...
entry.content: Do you have any updates on the timing of the report module being replaced? We are just starting the rollout of SAP CPQ and do not want to put effort into something that is not going to be available.

entry.title: Re: SAP B1 for HANA dependencies
entry.content:
Hi,

You can try public libraries from here:
https://software.opensuse.org/download.html?project=home%3Aawissu&package=compat-libgcrypt

or

https://software.opensuse.org/download/package?package=libgcrypt&project=openSUSE%3A12.3

entry.title: Re: can someone explain FSD in SAP ? what is role of fsd...
entry.content:
Hello, 

Please find below some other blogs/ community questions about usage of FSD:

https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blogs-by-members/functional-specification-document-fsd/ba-p/13874070

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-members/creating-a-functional-specification-document-fsd-in-sap-a-comprehensive/ba-p/13928511

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-q-a/how-to-review-functional-specification-document-fsd/qaq-p/6309521

Here is local program for Hungary, but may have some information regarding data that could be used in creating FSD for HCM:

https://help.sap.com/docs/ERP_HCM_SPV/8a38f318c29e4480a0d945f1d0bb1225/2b1f9753a321cc26e10000000a4450e5.html?locale=en-US

Regards, 

Jagoda

entry.title: Re: CRM Copy Badi Service contract to Order
entry.content:
Hi Laxmanakumar,

I will try this badi and confirm.The standard copy function module for refobj is not triggering in this case.

Regards

Niranjan

entry.title: Re: Maintenance plans with status "Scheduled Save to call "
entry.content:
Hi,

Technically, we can not identify the 'Scheduled to save' related maintenance plans as it is temporary status. When we schedule the maintenance plans there are two actions either save or exit without saving. Immediately after these actions, 'scheduled to save' status either gets reverted when we leave transaction without saving or gets converted to 'Scheduled called' upon saving the plan.

Simulations (IP19 / IP24) can be used to preempt the schedules.

Regards,

Sundar

entry.title: Re: CRM Copy Badi Service contract to Order
entry.content: Hi Robert, Yes it is stopping during the creation of service contract and not while creating the followup document.

entry.title: Re: SAP Developer Challenge March - SAP Community Escape House - AI 🤖🧠
entry.content:
Finally Completed

 

Great escape room @noravonthenen , waiting for more interesting challenge

entry.title: Re: SAP project system
entry.content: This is the wrong tag for this question. It is also a duplicate question and can be deleted.

entry.title: How do I hide columns in a sap.ui.mdc.table?
entry.content: I have a metadata-driven Fiori Elements list page build on top of a V4 OData service. This table is  sap.ui.mdc.table. When loading the list page, I need to hide a few columns based on certain criteria (e.g. if a user has some specific authorizations). I used .removeColumn(idx) method in onBeforeRendering extension of the list page controller, however, it didn't remove the column from the table personalization list, it only hid it from the list page table. Is there a way to hide it completely? Something like defining availability parameter as 'Hidden' in the manifest, but in the controller code

entry.title: Re: Increase of Hard Bounce Threshold in SMC
entry.content:
Dear Sasha,

Sinch Email 365 and SMC will not change any bounce management at this point since SMC is now only in maintenance mode officially until 31.12.2026. Those blogs might be useful.

SAP Marketing Cloud Bounces Management using Sinch E-mail 365 | SAP Blogs

SAP Marketing Cloud: Email Bounce Monitoring Best Practices | SAP Blogs

I understand that re-enabling a contact is a bit cumbersome. Unfortunately, we will not change this process at this time.

Best Regards, Isabelle Product Manager SAP Marketing Cloud.
